issues.description: 04-key_point.md lists 'True/False are special values (Booleans)' but booleans are not explicitly taught until M03. This is a forward reference that may confuse beginners encountering the concept for the first time in a key_point summary.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a parenthetical like '(you'll learn more about these in Module 3)' or defer to the data types lesson.

findings.lessonTitle: Different Kinds of Boxes (Data Types: Strings, Integers, Floats)

issues.description: 03-theory.md mentions 'IEEE 754 Standard: Python uses 64-bit precision for floats (about 15-17 decimal digits of accuracy)' which is overly technical for absolute beginners in a module labeled 'beginner' difficulty.
issues.suggestedFix: Move IEEE 754 detail to a collapsible 'Deep Dive' section or remove it — the practical implication (floating point imprecision) could be shown with a simple example like 0.1 + 0.2 instead.

findings.lessonTitle: Converting Between Data Types (Type Conversion & Casting)

issues.description: 02-example.md contains hardcoded 'current_year = 2025' in the Birth Year Calculator practical example. The year is already outdated.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with 'current_year = 2026' or better, use 'from datetime import datetime; current_year = datetime.now().year' to keep it evergreen.
issues.webResearchNote: Current year is 2026. The hardcoded 2025 value is outdated. Using datetime.now().year is the Python-idiomatic approach for dynamic year references.

issues.description: 02-example.md uses try/except for demonstrating TypeError, but try/except is not taught until M08 (Exception Handling). Beginners may be confused by the error handling syntax.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with a comment explaining what would happen, or use a simpler demonstration approach.

issues.description: starter.py contains bare assignments like 'hours = ' and 'minutes = ' which are Python syntax errors (SyntaxError: invalid syntax). Students cannot run the starter file to see the scaffolding before filling in their code.
issues.suggestedFix: Use placeholder values like 'hours = None  # Calculate using floor division (//)' or 'hours = 0  # TODO: calculate hours' so the starter file is at least syntactically valid.

issues.description: Challenge has only 1 test case ('Code runs without errors'). For a math-focused exercise, test cases verifying correct output for known inputs would strengthen validation.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases that verify '90 minutes → 1 hour and 30 minutes' output.

issues.description: starter.py contains bare assignments like 'name = ', 'choice = ', 'num1 = ', 'num2 = ', 'result = ' which are Python syntax errors. The file cannot be run or even parsed as valid Python.
issues.suggestedFix: Use placeholder values like 'name = None  # TODO: get user name' or empty strings/zeros so the file is syntactically valid.

issues.description: The mini-project uses if/elif/else statements extensively, but conditional statements are not taught until M03 (Boolean Logic). Students at this point in the curriculum have only learned about variables, data types, and operators.
issues.suggestedFix: Either restructure the calculator to use a simpler flow without conditionals, or add a note explaining that this is a preview of concepts from the next module.

issues.description: The calculator does not handle division by zero. The solution and starter code perform num1 / num2 and num1 // num2 without checking if num2 is zero.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a division-by-zero check or at least mention it as a consideration in the challenge description.
